revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro''' (交野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuzuha, Hirakata, Osaka|Kuzuha]], Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture. It is classified as a former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and is traditionally regarded as the site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace.
== Deities == * Main deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] ** [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}
== History == According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] and the records of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|岩清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}}, Emperor Kanmu established a ritual altar (kōshidan) in Katano District in 787 CE to enshrine his father, Emperor Kōnin. This is believed to be the origin of Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine">[https://osakadai3shibu.kilo.jp/jinja/hirakata/jinja/katanoamatukami.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association – Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro]</ref>
Initially known as Katano Amatsukami Shrine, the name changed over time to Kuzuha Shrine and then Tenjingu. In 1888, it was renamed Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The original tutelary deity (ujigami) of the area was the [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]], now a subsidiary shrine. When the shrine for Emperor Kōnin was established, it became the main shrine, relegating Kifune Shrine to subsidiary status. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), two separate shrines were built to enshrine Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Sugawara no Michizane. These were later re-integrated into Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro in 1872.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The shrine grounds, featuring lush primal forest, are traditionally associated with Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace. In 1984, the area was designated one of the "Eight Scenic Spots of Hirakata" by Hirakata City.<ref>[http://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/freepage/GYOUSEI/BUNKAN/work/hakkei.htm Hirakata City Cultural Spots]</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Hachiman-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * '''Main Hall (Important Cultural Property):''' Built in 1402 (Ōei 9), though some sources suggest construction during the Kamakura period, with repairs in 1238 and 1401. It is a single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing, and it retains features from the early Muromachi period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/186316|website=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * '''Worship Hall''' * '''Shrine Office'''
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''
<gallery> File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Kifune-jinja-2.jpg|Monument marking Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace site – Kifune Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Dai-jinja.jpg|Ōjinja Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, torii-2.jpg|Torii gate File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine grounds </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with two construction tags: Built in 1402 (Ōei 9). Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
== Location == * 2-19-1 Kuzuhaoka, Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture
== Access == * Approximately 2 km northeast of [[:en:Kuzuha Station|Kuzuha Station]] on the Keihan Main Line (32-minute walk).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat-inline|Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:大阪府の重要文化財
revision.text:
'''Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro''' (交野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuzuha, Hirakata, Osaka|Kuzuha]], Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture. It is classified as a former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and is traditionally regarded as the site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace.
== Deities == * Main deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] ** [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}
== History == According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] and the records of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|岩清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}}, Emperor Kanmu established a ritual altar (kōshidan) in Katano District in 787 CE to enshrine his father, Emperor Kōnin. This is believed to be the origin of Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine">[https://osakadai3shibu.kilo.jp/jinja/hirakata/jinja/katanoamatukami.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association – Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro]</ref>
Initially known as Katano Amatsukami Shrine, the name changed over time to Kuzuha Shrine and then Tenjingu. In 1888, it was renamed Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The original tutelary deity (ujigami) of the area was the [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]], now a subsidiary shrine. When the shrine for Emperor Kōnin was established, it became the main shrine, relegating Kifune Shrine to subsidiary status. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), two separate shrines were built to enshrine Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Sugawara no Michizane. These were later re-integrated into Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro in 1872.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The shrine grounds, featuring lush primal forest, are traditionally associated with Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace. In 1984, the area was designated one of the "Eight Scenic Spots of Hirakata" by Hirakata City.<ref>[http://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/freepage/GYOUSEI/BUNKAN/work/hakkei.htm Hirakata City Cultural Spots]</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Hachiman-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * '''Main Hall (Important Cultural Property):''' Built in 1402 (Ōei 9), though some sources suggest construction during the Kamakura period, with repairs in 1238 and 1401. It is a single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing, and it retains features from the early Muromachi period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/186316|website=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * '''Worship Hall''' * '''Shrine Office'''
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''
<gallery> File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Kifune-jinja-2.jpg|Monument marking Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace site – Kifune Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Dai-jinja.jpg|Ōjinja Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, torii-2.jpg|Torii gate File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine grounds </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with two construction tags: Built in 1402 (Ōei 9). Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
== Location == * 2-19-1 Kuzuhaoka, Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture
== Access == * Approximately 2 km northeast of [[:en:Kuzuha Station|Kuzuha Station]] on the Keihan Main Line (32-minute walk).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat-inline|Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:枚方市の建築物
revision.text:
'''Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro''' (交野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuzuha, Hirakata, Osaka|Kuzuha]], Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture. It is classified as a former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and is traditionally regarded as the site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace.
== Deities == * Main deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] ** [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}
== History == According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] and the records of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|岩清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}}, Emperor Kanmu established a ritual altar (kōshidan) in Katano District in 787 CE to enshrine his father, Emperor Kōnin. This is believed to be the origin of Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine">[https://osakadai3shibu.kilo.jp/jinja/hirakata/jinja/katanoamatukami.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association – Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro]</ref>
Initially known as Katano Amatsukami Shrine, the name changed over time to Kuzuha Shrine and then Tenjingu. In 1888, it was renamed Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The original tutelary deity (ujigami) of the area was the [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]], now a subsidiary shrine. When the shrine for Emperor Kōnin was established, it became the main shrine, relegating Kifune Shrine to subsidiary status. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), two separate shrines were built to enshrine Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Sugawara no Michizane. These were later re-integrated into Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro in 1872.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The shrine grounds, featuring lush primal forest, are traditionally associated with Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace. In 1984, the area was designated one of the "Eight Scenic Spots of Hirakata" by Hirakata City.<ref>[http://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/freepage/GYOUSEI/BUNKAN/work/hakkei.htm Hirakata City Cultural Spots]</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Hachiman-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * '''Main Hall (Important Cultural Property):''' Built in 1402 (Ōei 9), though some sources suggest construction during the Kamakura period, with repairs in 1238 and 1401. It is a single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing, and it retains features from the early Muromachi period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/186316|website=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * '''Worship Hall''' * '''Shrine Office'''
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''
<gallery> File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Kifune-jinja-2.jpg|Monument marking Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace site – Kifune Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Dai-jinja.jpg|Ōjinja Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, torii-2.jpg|Torii gate File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine grounds </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with two construction tags: Built in 1402 (Ōei 9). Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
== Location == * 2-19-1 Kuzuhaoka, Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture
== Access == * Approximately 2 km northeast of [[:en:Kuzuha Station|Kuzuha Station]] on the Keihan Main Line (32-minute walk).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat-inline|Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro}}

revision.comment: Bot: replace redirect category → Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture
revision.text:
'''Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro''' (交野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuzuha, Hirakata, Osaka|Kuzuha]], Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture. It is classified as a former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and is traditionally regarded as the site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace.
== Deities == * Main deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] ** [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}
== History == According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] and the records of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|岩清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}}, Emperor Kanmu established a ritual altar (kōshidan) in Katano District in 787 CE to enshrine his father, Emperor Kōnin. This is believed to be the origin of Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine">[https://osakadai3shibu.kilo.jp/jinja/hirakata/jinja/katanoamatukami.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association – Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro]</ref>
Initially known as Katano Amatsukami Shrine, the name changed over time to Kuzuha Shrine and then Tenjingu. In 1888, it was renamed Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The original tutelary deity (ujigami) of the area was the [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]], now a subsidiary shrine. When the shrine for Emperor Kōnin was established, it became the main shrine, relegating Kifune Shrine to subsidiary status. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), two separate shrines were built to enshrine Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Sugawara no Michizane. These were later re-integrated into Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro in 1872.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The shrine grounds, featuring lush primal forest, are traditionally associated with Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace. In 1984, the area was designated one of the "Eight Scenic Spots of Hirakata" by Hirakata City.<ref>[http://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/freepage/GYOUSEI/BUNKAN/work/hakkei.htm Hirakata City Cultural Spots]</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Hachiman-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * '''Main Hall (Important Cultural Property):''' Built in 1402 (Ōei 9), though some sources suggest construction during the Kamakura period, with repairs in 1238 and 1401. It is a single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing, and it retains features from the early Muromachi period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/186316|website=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * '''Worship Hall''' * '''Shrine Office'''
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''
<gallery> File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Kifune-jinja-2.jpg|Monument marking Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace site – Kifune Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Dai-jinja.jpg|Ōjinja Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, torii-2.jpg|Torii gate File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine grounds </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with two construction tags: Built in 1402 (Ōei 9). Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
== Location == * 2-19-1 Kuzuhaoka, Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture
== Access == * Approximately 2 km northeast of [[:en:Kuzuha Station|Kuzuha Station]] on the Keihan Main Line (32-minute walk).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat-inline|Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro}}

=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''
<gallery> File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Kifune-jinja-2.jpg|Monument marking Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace site – Kifune Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Dai-jinja.jpg|Ōjinja Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, torii-2.jpg|Torii gate File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine grounds </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with two construction tags: Built in 1402 (Ōei 9). Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
== Location == * 2-19-1 Kuzuhaoka, Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture
== Access == * Approximately 2 km northeast of [[:en:Kuzuha Station|Kuzuha Station]] on the Keihan Main Line (32-minute walk).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: Bot: update category → History of Hirakata, Osaka
revision.text:
'''Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro''' (交野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuzuha, Hirakata, Osaka|Kuzuha]], Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture. It is classified as a former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and is traditionally regarded as the site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace.
== Deities == * Main deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] ** [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}
== History == According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] and the records of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|岩清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}}, Emperor Kanmu established a ritual altar (kōshidan) in Katano District in 787 CE to enshrine his father, Emperor Kōnin. This is believed to be the origin of Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine">[https://osakadai3shibu.kilo.jp/jinja/hirakata/jinja/katanoamatukami.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association – Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro]</ref>
Initially known as Katano Amatsukami Shrine, the name changed over time to Kuzuha Shrine and then Tenjingu. In 1888, it was renamed Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The original tutelary deity (ujigami) of the area was the [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]], now a subsidiary shrine. When the shrine for Emperor Kōnin was established, it became the main shrine, relegating Kifune Shrine to subsidiary status. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), two separate shrines were built to enshrine Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Sugawara no Michizane. These were later re-integrated into Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro in 1872.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The shrine grounds, featuring lush primal forest, are traditionally associated with Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace. In 1984, the area was designated one of the "Eight Scenic Spots of Hirakata" by Hirakata City.<ref>[http://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/freepage/GYOUSEI/BUNKAN/work/hakkei.htm Hirakata City Cultural Spots]</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Hachiman-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * '''Main Hall (Important Cultural Property):''' Built in 1402 (Ōei 9), though some sources suggest construction during the Kamakura period, with repairs in 1238 and 1401. It is a single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing, and it retains features from the early Muromachi period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/186316|website=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * '''Worship Hall''' * '''Shrine Office'''
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''
<gallery> File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Kifune-jinja-2.jpg|Monument marking Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace site – Kifune Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Dai-jinja.jpg|Ōjinja Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, torii-2.jpg|Torii gate File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine grounds </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with two construction tags: Built in 1402 (Ōei 9). Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
== Location == * 2-19-1 Kuzuhaoka, Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture
== Access == * Approximately 2 km northeast of [[:en:Kuzuha Station|Kuzuha Station]] on the Keihan Main Line (32-minute walk).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat-inline|Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro}}

This is believed to be the origin of Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine">[https://osakadai3shibu.kilo.jp/jinja/hirakata/jinja/katanoamatukami.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association – Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro]</ref>
Initially known as Katano Amatsukami Shrine, the name changed over time to Kuzuha Shrine and then Tenjingu. In 1888, it was renamed Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The original tutelary deity (ujigami) of the area was the [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]], now a subsidiary shrine. When the shrine for Emperor Kōnin was established, it became the main shrine, relegating Kifune Shrine to subsidiary status. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), two separate shrines were built to enshrine Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Sugawara no Michizane. These were later re-integrated into Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro in 1872.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The shrine grounds, featuring lush primal forest, are traditionally associated with Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace. In 1984, the area was designated one of the "Eight Scenic Spots of Hirakata" by Hirakata City.<ref>[http://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/freepage/GYOUSEI/BUNKAN/work/hakkei.htm Hirakata City Cultural Spots]</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Hachiman-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * '''Main Hall (Important Cultural Property):''' Built in 1402 (Ōei 9), though some sources suggest construction during the Kamakura period, with repairs in 1238 and 1401. It is a single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing, and it retains features from the early Muromachi period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/186316|website=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * '''Worship Hall''' * '''Shrine Office'''
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''
<gallery> File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Kifune-jinja-2.jpg|Monument marking Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace site – Kifune Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Dai-jinja.jpg|Ōjinja Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, torii-2.jpg|Torii gate File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine grounds </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with two construction tags: Built in 1402 (Ōei 9). Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
== Location == * 2-19-1 Kuzuhaoka, Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture
== Access == * Approximately 2 km northeast of [[:en:Kuzuha Station|Kuzuha Station]] on the Keihan Main Line (32-minute walk).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat-inline|Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro}}

'''Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro''' (交野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuzuha, Hirakata, Osaka|Kuzuha]], Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture. It is classified as a former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and is traditionally regarded as the site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace.
== Deities == * Main deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] ** [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}
== History == According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] and the records of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|岩清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}}, Emperor Kanmu established a ritual altar (kōshidan) in Katano District in 787 CE to enshrine his father, Emperor Kōnin. This is believed to be the origin of Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine">[https://osakadai3shibu.kilo.jp/jinja/hirakata/jinja/katanoamatukami.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association – Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro]</ref>
Initially known as Katano Amatsukami Shrine, the name changed over time to Kuzuha Shrine and then Tenjingu. In 1888, it was renamed Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The original tutelary deity (ujigami) of the area was the [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]], now a subsidiary shrine. When the shrine for Emperor Kōnin was established, it became the main shrine, relegating Kifune Shrine to subsidiary status. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), two separate shrines were built to enshrine Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Sugawara no Michizane. These were later re-integrated into Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro in 1872.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The shrine grounds, featuring lush primal forest, are traditionally associated with Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace. In 1984, the area was designated one of the "Eight Scenic Spots of Hirakata" by Hirakata City.<ref>[http://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/freepage/GYOUSEI/BUNKAN/work/hakkei.htm Hirakata City Cultural Spots]</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Hachiman-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * '''Main Hall (Important Cultural Property):''' Built in 1402 (Ōei 9), though some sources suggest construction during the Kamakura period, with repairs in 1238 and 1401. It is a single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing, and it retains features from the early Muromachi period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/186316|website=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * '''Worship Hall''' * '''Shrine Office'''
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''

== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with two construction tags: Built in 1402 (Ōei 9). Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>

'''Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro''' (交野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuzuha, Hirakata, Osaka|Kuzuha]], Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture. It is classified as a former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and is traditionally regarded as the site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace.
== Deities == * Main deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] ** [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] and the records of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|岩清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮}}, Emperor Kanmu established a ritual altar (kōshidan) in Katano District in 787 CE to enshrine his father, Emperor Kōnin. This is believed to be the origin of Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine">[https://osakadai3shibu.kilo.jp/jinja/hirakata/jinja/katanoamatukami.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association – Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro]</ref>
Initially known as Katano Amatsukami Shrine, the name changed over time to Kuzuha Shrine and then Tenjingu. In 1888, it was renamed Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The original tutelary deity (ujigami) of the area was the [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]], now a subsidiary shrine. When the shrine for Emperor Kōnin was established, it became the main shrine, relegating Kifune Shrine to subsidiary status. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), two separate shrines were built to enshrine Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Sugawara no Michizane. These were later re-integrated into Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro in 1872.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The shrine grounds, featuring lush primal forest, are traditionally associated with Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace. In 1984, the area was designated one of the "Eight Scenic Spots of Hirakata" by Hirakata City.<ref>[http://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/freepage/GYOUSEI/BUNKAN/work/hakkei.htm Hirakata City Cultural Spots]</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Hachiman-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * '''Main Hall (Important Cultural Property):''' Built in 1402 (Ōei 9), though some sources suggest construction during the Kamakura period, with repairs in 1238 and 1401. It is a single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing, and it retains features from the early Muromachi period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/186316|website=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * '''Worship Hall''' * '''Shrine Office'''
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''
<gallery> File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Kifune-jinja-2.jpg|Monument marking Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace site – Kifune Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Dai-jinja.jpg|Ōjinja Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, torii-2.jpg|Torii gate File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine grounds </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with two construction tags: Built in 1402 (Ōei 9). Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
== Location == * 2-19-1 Kuzuhaoka, Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture
== Access == * Approximately 2 km northeast of [[:en:Kuzuha Station|Kuzuha Station]] on the Keihan Main Line (32-minute walk).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat-inline|Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro}}

revision.text:
'''Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro''' (交野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuzuha, Hirakata, Osaka|Kuzuha]], Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture. It is classified as a former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and is traditionally regarded as the site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace.
== Deities == * Main deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] ** [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] and the records of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|岩清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮}}, Emperor Kanmu established a ritual altar (kōshidan) in Katano District in 787 CE to enshrine his father, Emperor Kōnin. This is believed to be the origin of Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine">[https://osakadai3shibu.kilo.jp/jinja/hirakata/jinja/katanoamatukami.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association – Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro]</ref>
Initially known as Katano Amatsukami Shrine, the name changed over time to Kuzuha Shrine and then Tenjingu. In 1888, it was renamed Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The original tutelary deity (ujigami) of the area was the [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]], now a subsidiary shrine. When the shrine for Emperor Kōnin was established, it became the main shrine, relegating Kifune Shrine to subsidiary status. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), two separate shrines were built to enshrine Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Sugawara no Michizane. These were later re-integrated into Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro in 1872.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The shrine grounds, featuring lush primal forest, are traditionally associated with Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace. In 1984, the area was designated one of the "Eight Scenic Spots of Hirakata" by Hirakata City.<ref>[http://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/freepage/GYOUSEI/BUNKAN/work/hakkei.htm Hirakata City Cultural Spots]</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Hachiman-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * '''Main Hall (Important Cultural Property):''' Built in 1402 (Ōei 9), though some sources suggest construction during the Kamakura period, with repairs in 1238 and 1401. It is a single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing, and it retains features from the early Muromachi period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/186316|website=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * '''Worship Hall''' * '''Shrine Office'''
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''
<gallery> File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Kifune-jinja-2.jpg|Monument marking Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace site – Kifune Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Dai-jinja.jpg|Ōjinja Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, torii-2.jpg|Torii gate File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine grounds </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with two construction tags: Built in 1402 (Ōei 9). Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
== Location == * 2-19-1 Kuzuhaoka, Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture
== Access == * Approximately 2 km northeast of [[:en:Kuzuha Station|Kuzuha Station]] on the Keihan Main Line (32-minute walk).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat-inline|Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro}}

Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro]][[ja:交野天神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11374034]] Wikidata name: Katano Tenjinsha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11374034]] Wikidata name: Katano Tenjinsha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro''' (交野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuzuha, Hirakata, Osaka|Kuzuha]], Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture. It is classified as a former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and is traditionally regarded as the site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace.
== Deities == * Main deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] ** [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
== History == According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] and the records of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|岩清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮}}, Emperor Kanmu established a ritual altar (kōshidan) in Katano District in 787 CE to enshrine his father, Emperor Kōnin. This is believed to be the origin of Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine">[https://osakadai3shibu.kilo.jp/jinja/hirakata/jinja/katanoamatukami.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association – Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro]</ref>
Initially known as Katano Amatsukami Shrine, the name changed over time to Kuzuha Shrine and then Tenjingu. In 1888, it was renamed Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The original tutelary deity (ujigami) of the area was the [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]], now a subsidiary shrine. When the shrine for Emperor Kōnin was established, it became the main shrine, relegating Kifune Shrine to subsidiary status. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), two separate shrines were built to enshrine Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Sugawara no Michizane. These were later re-integrated into Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro in 1872.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The shrine grounds, featuring lush primal forest, are traditionally associated with Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace. In 1984, the area was designated one of the "Eight Scenic Spots of Hirakata" by Hirakata City.<ref>[http://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/freepage/GYOUSEI/BUNKAN/work/hakkei.htm Hirakata City Cultural Spots]</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Hachiman-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * '''Main Hall (Important Cultural Property):''' Built in 1402 (Ōei 9), though some sources suggest construction during the Kamakura period, with repairs in 1238 and 1401. It is a single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing, and it retains features from the early Muromachi period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/186316|website=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * '''Worship Hall''' * '''Shrine Office'''
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''

Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro]][[ja:交野天神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11374034]] Wikidata name: Katano Tenjinsha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11374034]] Wikidata name: Katano Tenjinsha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro''' (交野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuzuha, Hirakata, Osaka|Kuzuha]], Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture. It is classified as a former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and is traditionally regarded as the site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace.
== Deities == * Main deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] ** [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
== History == According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] and the records of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|岩清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮}}, Emperor Kanmu established a ritual altar (kōshidan) in Katano District in 787 CE to enshrine his father, Emperor Kōnin. This is believed to be the origin of Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine">[https://osakadai3shibu.kilo.jp/jinja/hirakata/jinja/katanoamatukami.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association – Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro]</ref>
Initially known as Katano Amatsukami Shrine, the name changed over time to Kuzuha Shrine and then Tenjingu. In 1888, it was renamed Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The original tutelary deity (ujigami) of the area was the [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]], now a subsidiary shrine. When the shrine for Emperor Kōnin was established, it became the main shrine, relegating Kifune Shrine to subsidiary status. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), two separate shrines were built to enshrine Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Sugawara no Michizane. These were later re-integrated into Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro in 1872.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The shrine grounds, featuring lush primal forest, are traditionally associated with Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace. In 1984, the area was designated one of the "Eight Scenic Spots of Hirakata" by Hirakata City.<ref>[http://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/freepage/GYOUSEI/BUNKAN/work/hakkei.htm Hirakata City Cultural Spots]</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Hachiman-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * '''Main Hall (Important Cultural Property):''' Built in 1402 (Ōei 9), though some sources suggest construction during the Kamakura period, with repairs in 1238 and 1401. It is a single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing, and it retains features from the early Muromachi period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/186316|website=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * '''Worship Hall''' * '''Shrine Office'''
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''
<gallery> File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Kifune-jinja-2.jpg|Monument marking Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace site – Kifune Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Dai-jinja.jpg|Ōjinja Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, torii-2.jpg|Torii gate File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine grounds </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with two construction tags: Built in 1402 (Ōei 9). Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
== Location == * 2-19-1 Kuzuhaoka, Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture
== Access == * Approximately 2 km northeast of [[:en:Kuzuha Station|Kuzuha Station]] on the Keihan Main Line (32-minute walk).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

== Location == * 2-19-1 Kuzuhaoka, Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture
== Access == * Approximately 2 km northeast of [[:en:Kuzuha Station|Kuzuha Station]] on the Keihan Main Line (32-minute walk).

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro]][[ja:交野天神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11374034]] Wikidata name: Katano Tenjinsha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11374034]] Wikidata name: Katano Tenjinsha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro''' (交野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuzuha, Hirakata, Osaka|Kuzuha]], Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture. It is classified as a former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and is traditionally regarded as the site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace.
== Deities == * Main deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] ** [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
== History == According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] and the records of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|岩清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮}}, Emperor Kanmu established a ritual altar (kōshidan) in Katano District in 787 CE to enshrine his father, Emperor Kōnin. This is believed to be the origin of Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine">[https://osakadai3shibu.kilo.jp/jinja/hirakata/jinja/katanoamatukami.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association – Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro]</ref>
Initially known as Katano Amatsukami Shrine, the name changed over time to Kuzuha Shrine and then Tenjingu. In 1888, it was renamed Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The original tutelary deity (ujigami) of the area was the [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]], now a subsidiary shrine. When the shrine for Emperor Kōnin was established, it became the main shrine, relegating Kifune Shrine to subsidiary status. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), two separate shrines were built to enshrine Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Sugawara no Michizane. These were later re-integrated into Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro in 1872.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The shrine grounds, featuring lush primal forest, are traditionally associated with Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace. In 1984, the area was designated one of the "Eight Scenic Spots of Hirakata" by Hirakata City.<ref>[http://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/freepage/GYOUSEI/BUNKAN/work/hakkei.htm Hirakata City Cultural Spots]</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Hachiman-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * '''Main Hall (Important Cultural Property):''' Built in 1402 (Ōei 9), though some sources suggest construction during the Kamakura period, with repairs in 1238 and 1401. It is a single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing, and it retains features from the early Muromachi period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/186316|website=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * '''Worship Hall''' * '''Shrine Office'''
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''
<gallery> File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Kifune-jinja-2.jpg|Monument marking Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace site – Kifune Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Dai-jinja.jpg|Ōjinja Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, torii-2.jpg|Torii gate File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine grounds </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with two construction tags: Built in 1402 (Ōei 9). Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
== Location == * 2-19-1 Kuzuhaoka, Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture
== Access == * Approximately 2 km northeast of [[:en:Kuzuha Station|Kuzuha Station]] on the Keihan Main Line (32-minute walk).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat-inline|Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro}}

== History == According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] and the records of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|岩清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮}}, Emperor Kanmu established a ritual altar (kōshidan) in Katano District in 787 CE to enshrine his father, Emperor Kōnin. This is believed to be the origin of Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine">[https://osakadai3shibu.kilo.jp/jinja/hirakata/jinja/katanoamatukami.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association – Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro]</ref>
Initially known as Katano Amatsukami Shrine, the name changed over time to Kuzuha Shrine and then Tenjingu. In 1888, it was renamed Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The original tutelary deity (ujigami) of the area was the [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]], now a subsidiary shrine. When the shrine for Emperor Kōnin was established, it became the main shrine, relegating Kifune Shrine to subsidiary status. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), two separate shrines were built to enshrine Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Sugawara no Michizane. These were later re-integrated into Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro in 1872.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The shrine grounds, featuring lush primal forest, are traditionally associated with Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace. In 1984, the area was designated one of the "Eight Scenic Spots of Hirakata" by Hirakata City.<ref>[http://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/freepage/GYOUSEI/BUNKAN/work/hakkei.htm Hirakata City Cultural Spots]</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Hachiman-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * '''Main Hall (Important Cultural Property):''' Built in 1402 (Ōei 9), though some sources suggest construction during the Kamakura period, with repairs in 1238 and 1401. It is a single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing, and it retains features from the early Muromachi period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/186316|website=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * '''Worship Hall''' * '''Shrine Office'''
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''
<gallery> File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Kifune-jinja-2.jpg|Monument marking Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace site – Kifune Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Dai-jinja.jpg|Ōjinja Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, torii-2.jpg|Torii gate File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine grounds </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with two construction tags: Built in 1402 (Ōei 9). Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
== Location == * 2-19-1 Kuzuhaoka, Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture
== Access == * Approximately 2 km northeast of [[:en:Kuzuha Station|Kuzuha Station]] on the Keihan Main Line (32-minute walk).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro]][[ja:交野天神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11374034]] Wikidata name: Katano Tenjinsha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11374034]] Wikidata name: Katano Tenjinsha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro''' (交野天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kuzuha, Hirakata, Osaka|Kuzuha]], Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture. It is classified as a former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and is traditionally regarded as the site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace.
== Deities == * Main deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] ** [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
== History == According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] and the records of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|岩清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮}}, Emperor Kanmu established a ritual altar (kōshidan) in Katano District in 787 CE to enshrine his father, Emperor Kōnin. This is believed to be the origin of Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine">[https://osakadai3shibu.kilo.jp/jinja/hirakata/jinja/katanoamatukami.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association – Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro]</ref>
Initially known as Katano Amatsukami Shrine, the name changed over time to Kuzuha Shrine and then Tenjingu. In 1888, it was renamed Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The original tutelary deity (ujigami) of the area was the [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]], now a subsidiary shrine. When the shrine for Emperor Kōnin was established, it became the main shrine, relegating Kifune Shrine to subsidiary status. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), two separate shrines were built to enshrine Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Sugawara no Michizane. These were later re-integrated into Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro in 1872.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The shrine grounds, featuring lush primal forest, are traditionally associated with Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace. In 1984, the area was designated one of the "Eight Scenic Spots of Hirakata" by Hirakata City.<ref>[http://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/freepage/GYOUSEI/BUNKAN/work/hakkei.htm Hirakata City Cultural Spots]</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Hachiman-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * '''Main Hall (Important Cultural Property):''' Built in 1402 (Ōei 9), though some sources suggest construction during the Kamakura period, with repairs in 1238 and 1401. It is a single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing, and it retains features from the early Muromachi period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/186316|website=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * '''Worship Hall''' * '''Shrine Office'''
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''
<gallery> File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Kifune-jinja-2.jpg|Monument marking Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace site – Kifune Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Dai-jinja.jpg|Ōjinja Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, torii-2.jpg|Torii gate File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine grounds </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with two construction tags: Built in 1402 (Ōei 9). Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
== Location == * 2-19-1 Kuzuhaoka, Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture
== Access == * Approximately 2 km northeast of [[:en:Kuzuha Station|Kuzuha Station]] on the Keihan Main Line (32-minute walk).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat-inline|Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro}}

This is believed to be the origin of Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine">[https://osakadai3shibu.kilo.jp/jinja/hirakata/jinja/katanoamatukami.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association – Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro]</ref>
Initially known as Katano Amatsukami Shrine, the name changed over time to Kuzuha Shrine and then Tenjingu. In 1888, it was renamed Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The original tutelary deity (ujigami) of the area was the [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]], now a subsidiary shrine. When the shrine for Emperor Kōnin was established, it became the main shrine, relegating Kifune Shrine to subsidiary status. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), two separate shrines were built to enshrine Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Sugawara no Michizane. These were later re-integrated into Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro in 1872.<ref name="Katano Shrine" />
The shrine grounds, featuring lush primal forest, are traditionally associated with Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace. In 1984, the area was designated one of the "Eight Scenic Spots of Hirakata" by Hirakata City.<ref>[http://www.city.hirakata.osaka.jp/freepage/GYOUSEI/BUNKAN/work/hakkei.htm Hirakata City Cultural Spots]</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Hachiman-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * '''Main Hall (Important Cultural Property):''' Built in 1402 (Ōei 9), though some sources suggest construction during the Kamakura period, with repairs in 1238 and 1401. It is a single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing, and it retains features from the early Muromachi period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/186316|website=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * '''Worship Hall''' * '''Shrine Office'''
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * '''Kifune Shrine (Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property):''' A single-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress-bark roofing.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Survey Report on Hirakata City Religious Buildings III |publisher=Hirakata City Board of Education |date=1994}}</ref> * '''Hachiman Shrine (Important Cultural Property):''' Similar in construction to the main hall but slightly smaller. Built during the Kamakura period, it features intricate carvings on the decorative transoms. * '''Ōjinja Shrine'''
<gallery> File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Kifune-jinja-2.jpg|Monument marking Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace site – Kifune Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, Dai-jinja.jpg|Ōjinja Shrine File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, torii-2.jpg|Torii gate File:Katanoamatsukami-no-yashiro, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the shrine grounds </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with two construction tags: Built in 1402 (Ōei 9). Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2169|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Main Hall}}</ref> * Hachiman Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 1917.<ref>{{National Heritage Database|102|2170|Katano Amatsukami-no-Yashiro Hachiman Shrine}}</ref>
=== Osaka Prefectural Historic Site === * Site of Emperor Keitai's Kuzuha Palace (designated in 1971).<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
=== Hirakata City Tangible Cultural Property === * Kifune Shrine Main Hall: Designated in 2004.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/attach/2824/00072558/013hirakataR1.pdf Osaka Prefectural Historic Sites List]}}</ref>
== Location == * 2-19-1 Kuzuhaoka, Hirakata City, Osaka Prefecture
== Access == * Approximately 2 km northeast of [[:en:Kuzuha Station|Kuzuha Station]] on the Keihan Main Line (32-minute walk).
